page: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-3xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server configured on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 32 shared vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The server is equipped with three NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture family, providing a combined 72 GB of VRAM.
This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning storage resources must be attached externally. For connectivity, the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of 32 vCPUs, a large 384.0 GB memory footprint, and three enterprise-grade NVIDIA L4 GPUs offers a dense resource profile for parallel computing tasks without the overhead of dedicated CPU allocations. This hardware profile is suited for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science pipelines, video transcoding, and graphics rendering tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-32xCPU-384GB-3xL4 is a KVM-based virtual server designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. It features 32 shared vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, which provides 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The primary hardware highlight is the inclusion of three NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace family, delivering a total of 72 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external storage attachments. It comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address. Given its high memory capacity and triple-GPU configuration, this server is suited for parallel processing tasks, including machine learning inference, data science, and video processing.
